props.title: Workflow Server
props.description: Workflow Server is a key component for managing the lifecycle of business objects within your enterprise.

props.subtitle: Overview
props.title: Deploy a workflow backend in minutes
props.description: Workflow Server is a ready-to-use Workflow Engine-based application that you can deploy into your infrastructure. It can be integrated with any applications via a REST API. Perfect for microservices-based architecture.

props.subtitle: Why Workflow Server
props.title: Run workflows without embedding code
props.description: Everything Workflow Engine offers, wrapped in a deployable application with a REST API and an admin panel.

features.title: Ready-to-use
features.text: 100% ready to run. Launch it and start creating processes via HTTP API straight away — no host application required.

features.title: Full-featured & high performance
features.text: Covers every workflow case — including document approval — and delivers reliable performance under production load.

features.title: REST API
features.text: Manage workflows with simple GET and POST requests. Process instance creation, command execution, state queries, inbox / outbox — all over HTTP.

features.title: Cross-platform
features.text: Runs on Windows, Linux, and macOS. Ships as a console application, Windows Service, or Docker container.

props.title: API
props.description: The set of APIs enables the exchange of messages between external applications and Workflow Server via the HTTP protocol.

features.title: Workflow API
features.text: Workflow API enables you to manage everything related to your schemes and workflows, be it the creation of a process instance, returning the list of available commands and executing them, getting the list of available states and setting them, inbox/outbox folders, etc.
bullets: Getting a list of commands & executing them
Getting a list of available states & setting them

features.title: Callback API
features.text: Callback API allows you to integrate Workflow Server into any infrastructure (for example, the one based on microservice architecture) by specifying external URLs that you wish Workflow Server to call whenever a specific event occurs.
bullets: Getting a list of actions & executing them
Getting a list of conditions & executing them

props.subtitle: Deployment
props.title: Runs anywhere your stack lives
props.description: Minimum specs for running Workflow Server in production.

features.title: Operating system
features.text: Windows, Linux, or macOS. Docker container deployment fully supported.

features.title: Supported databases
features.text: Microsoft SQL Server, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL, and MongoDB.

features.title: Minimum hardware
features.text: 1 CPU core @ 1 GHz, 1 GB RAM, 5 GB disk. Scales horizontally to match your workload.

features.title: Application modes
features.text: Run as a standalone console application or as a Windows Service — choose whichever fits your deployment model.

props.title: Ready to try it?
props.description: Download Workflow Server and get a cross-platform workflow backend running in minutes.
